arguments.task: Open and read official Binance developer documentation pages that support these fields for Binance API research: one-line product description, authentication methods, access/pricing or self-serve availability, API surface type and breadth, WebSocket/FIX if documented, rate limits, and whether Binance documents an MCP server. Prefer first-party pages under developers.binance.com or binance-docs.github.io and official GitHub repositories owned by Binance. Return the final answer with the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted findings tied to each URL. Do not rely on search snippets. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for it to load and extract the visible text. Focus on Spot API docs, auth/request security docs, rate limits/general API info, and any official MCP documentation or repository.

input.thought: Use browser automation to inspect official Binance sources directly and extract evidence-backed documentation details.

raw_result.description: Binance provides developer APIs for crypto trading and market data across Spot and related products.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Binance Spot REST docs describe API key authentication via the X-MBX-APIKEY header for secure endpoints, and signed requests using HMAC, RSA, or Ed25519 keys/signatures depending on key type.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official public developer documentation is openly accessible without contact-sales or partner approval, indicating self-serve developer access to start building against public APIs.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs and gathered evidence show a broad API surface including Spot REST API plus separately documented WebSocket API, WebSocket Streams, and FIX API in the Binance developer portal.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Gathered browser evidence indicates official Binance developer portal search results mentioning MCP-compatible AI agents and a GitHub repository at github.com/binance/binance-mcp-server, but the repository page itself was not read in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Authentication for trading endpoints requires manual creation/configuration of Binance API keys and request signing setup (HMAC/RSA/Ed25519), rather than simple OAuth.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence from the Spot REST API docs indicates IP-based request-weight limits and unfilled order count limits, with HTTP 429 on limit violations and HTTP 418 bans for repeated abuse; Retry-After headers are documented.
raw_result.notable_signals: The Binance developer portal appears comprehensive and modern, with dedicated sections for REST API, WebSocket API, WebSocket Streams, FIX API, and agent-native developer resources.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field api_surface.mcp_exists cannot be fully supported from the gathered evidence because the browser run only noted portal search results and a likely GitHub repository name for MCP, but did not read a canonical official MCP page or repository content directly. Additionally, api_surface.type may understate the surface because the evidence notes WebSocket and FIX alongside REST, but the allowed schema only permits one of REST, GraphQL, Both, or None.
